blocks.text: The data model should make every visual decision reproducible: a widget is a rectangle in grid units, not a pile of DOM styles. Pixels are derived from the grid config at render time.

blocks.title: What each field buys you
rows: id
Stable identity across rerenders, persistence, and widget data joins.
A reordered dashboard can attach saved size/position to the wrong widget.
x, y
Logical grid origin for snap, collision, and responsive remapping.
Raw pixels become stale when container width or column count changes.
width, height
Logical rectangle size in columns/rows.
Resize logic cannot enforce grid-aligned collisions.
minWidth, minHeight
Prevents charts, controls, or tables from becoming unusable.
Resize handles can create broken or inaccessible widget states.
version
Lets the app migrate old snapshots when fields or breakpoints change.
Old saved layouts either break silently or require risky one-off parsing.

blocks.title: Persistent vs transient state
rows: Persistent layout
id, x, y, width, height, min constraints, version, breakpoint key.
localStorage or server snapshot; restored on page load.
Transient interaction
activeId, pointer start, candidate rectangle, keyboard step, placeholder position.
Component/controller memory only; never persisted.
Widget runtime state
Chart filters, table sort, data loading, error state.
Owned by widget or data layer; not mixed into layout collision math.
Persistence status
saving, saved, failed, stale server version.
Dashboard shell or sync adapter; visible to users without blocking drag.

steps.title: 1. Load
steps.text: Read the newest compatible snapshot for the user and breakpoint; fall back to a default layout if missing or invalid.

steps.title: 2. Migrate
steps.text: If snapshot.version is older, transform field names, clamp invalid sizes, and fill missing breakpoint layouts before rendering.

steps.title: 3. Edit locally
steps.text: During drag/resize, update candidate layout locally and keep visual feedback immediate.

steps.title: 4. Commit
steps.text: On pointerup, keyboard confirm, or explicit Save, write a new snapshot and mark the local layout as pending/saved.

steps.title: 5. Resolve conflict
steps.text: If a server layout has a newer version, choose a product rule: last-write-wins, prompt the user, or keep per-device layouts separate.

blocks.title: Responsive layout policy
rows: One desktop layout scaled down
Simple persistence and predictable desktop editing.
Mobile can become a cramped stack unless you derive a separate mobile order.
Separate layouts per breakpoint
Best user control for desktop, tablet, and mobile.
More snapshot data and migration paths.
Desktop editable, mobile read-only stacked order
Good for dashboards where drag/resize is mainly a desktop workflow.
Mobile users need a separate reorder/settings interface if editing is required.

blocks.title: Data-model signal
blocks.text: Persist the smallest stable layout contract. Do not persist pointer positions, DOM measurements, isDragging flags, or chart runtime state inside the layout snapshot.
